questions.question:
What is printed by following program, assuming call-by reference method of passing parameters for all variables in the parameter list of procedure P?

program     Main(inout, output);
var     a, b:integer;
        procedure P(x, y, z:integer);
        begin
            y:=y+1
            z:=x+x
        end P;
begin
    a:=2; b:=3;
    p(a+b, a, a);
    Write(a)
end.


GATE CSE 1988 | Question: 2xv

In which of the following case(s) is it possible to obtain different results for call-by-reference and call-by-name parameter passing?



GATE CSE 1989 | Question: 3-viii
options: Passing as array element as a parameter
correct_answer: Passing as array element as a parameter

questions.question:
Indicate all the true statements from the following:



GATE CSE 1991 | Question: 03,x
options: Recursive descent parsing cannot be used for grammar with left recursion.
The intermediate form for representing expressions which is best suited for code optimization is the postfix form.
A programming language not supporting either recursion or pointer type does not need the support of dynamic memory allocation.
Although C does not support call-by-name parameter passing, the effect can be correctly simulated in C
No feature of Pascal typing violates strong typing in Pascal.
correct_answer: Recursive descent parsing cannot be used for grammar with left recursion.
Although C does not support call-by-name parameter passing, the effect can be correctly simulated in C

questions.question:
What is the value of \( X \) printed by the following program?

program COMPUTE (input, output);
var X:integer;
procedure FIND (X:real);
    begin
        X:=sqrt(X);
    end;
begin
    X:=2
    FIND(X);
    writeln(X);
end.


GATE CSE 1995 | Question: 2.4

Consider the following function

void swap(int a, int b)  
{       
    int temp;
    temp = a;
    a = b;
    b = temp;  
}

In order to exchange the values of two variables \( x \) and \( y \).



GATE CSE 2004 | Question: 2,ISRO2017-54
options: \( swap (x, y) \) cannot be used as it does not return any value
\( swap (x, y) \) cannot be used as the parameters are passed by value
questions.correct_answer: \( swap (x, y) \) cannot be used as the parameters are passed by value

questions.question:
What will be the output of the following pseudo-code when parameters are passed by reference and dynamic scoping is assumed?                

a = 3;
void n(x) { x = x * a; print (x); }
void m(y) { a = 1 ; a = y - a;  n(a); print (a); }
void main () { m(a); }


GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 36

Consider the program below in a hypothetical language which allows global variable and a choice of call by reference or call by value methods of parameter passing.         

int i ;
program main ()
{
    int j = 60;
    i = 50;
    call f (i, j);
    print i, j;
}
procedure f (x, y)
{           
    i = 100;
    x = 10;
    y = y + i ;
}

Which one of the following options represents the correct output of the program for the two parameter passing mechanisms?



GATE IT 2007 | Question: 33
options: Call by value : \( i = 70, j = 10 \); Call by reference : \( i = 60, j = 70 \)
Call by value : \( i = 50, j = 60 \); Call by reference : \( i = 50, j = 70 \)
Call by value : \( i = 10, j = 70 \); Call by reference : \( i = 100, j = 60 \)
Call by value : \( i = 100, j = 60 \); Call by reference : \( i = 10, j = 70 \)
questions.correct_answer: Call by value : \( i = 100, j = 60 \); Call by reference : \( i = 10, j = 70 \)
